toolUseResult.content:
# Slice 3.4 — Real VM Server, ELF Loader, Cross-AS IPC (Phase 3 milestone)

## Context

Phase 3 delivers the milestone **"Boot processes each have isolated address
spaces; VM handles page faults."** Slices 3.1a–3.3 (all merged) built the
physical frame allocator, per-process TTBR0s + ASIDs, a real EL0 page-fault
handler with `RTS_PAGEFAULT`, and the six real `SYS_VMCTL` subcalls. Today the
kernel itself plays VM: `do_page_fault` resolves heap-window faults inline
(`exception.rs:219-244`), and there is **no user-space process** — every EL0
"process" is a hand-coded assembly blob (`user_stub.S`) copied into one page.

Slice 3.4 lifts memory management out of the kernel and into the first **real
user-space server**: the VM server, loaded from an ELF, running its own
receive loop, resolving faults via `SYS_VMCTL`. Reaching it requires
infrastructure that **does not exist yet**: a user-space build toolchain
(linker script, real `minix-ipc` SVC stubs, a freestanding ELF), a kernel ELF
loader, and an embed mechanism so `cargo run -p minix4-kernel` stays the single
build command.

This is the heaviest slice in the project, so it ships as **two reviewable
PRs**: **3.4a** (build toolchain + ELF loader, proven by a spin-loop VM) and
**3.4b** (real VM fault loop + kernel→VM page-fault send + stub-D revert — the
milestone). The cross-AS user-copy rewrite the petal plan lists is **deferred**:
with `schedule_next` (3.1b) flushing `MF_DELIVERMSG` *after* the TTBR0 switch,
every user-buffer copy in 3.4 already runs under the correct live TTBR0, so a
walk-based copy is not yet needed and is left to Phase 4.

**Doc reconciliation (do first, in 3.4a's PR):** `docs/plan.md` still marks
slice 3.3 `◀ ready (branch …, pending merge)` but PR #12 merged it
(`git log`: `64c9548`). Flip 3.3 to `✓ shipped (PR #12, merged 2026-05-28)` and
keep `3.4 ◀ next`.

---

## Build / embed strategy (Option 1 — `build.rs` shells out, `include_bytes!`)

The kernel `build.rs` already shells out to `clang` for `.S` files. Extend it to:

1. Run `cargo build -p minix4-vm --target aarch64-unknown-none --release` with
   **`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$OUT_DIR/vm-target`** (a separate target dir — avoids the
   workspace build-lock deadlock from nesting cargo inside a build script of the
   same workspace) and **`env_remove("RUSTFLAGS")`** + an inner
   `--config target.aarch64-unknown-none.rustflags=["-C","link-arg=-Tservers/vm/user.ld"]`
   so the VM binary links with the **user** linker script, not the kernel's
   (`.cargo/config.toml` otherwise forces `-Tkernel/.../linker.ld` on that triple).
2. `println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=servers/vm/...")` so the kernel rebuilds when
   VM changes.
3. Expose the produced ELF path to the kernel and `include_bytes!` it into a
   rodata `&[u8]` (new `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs`: `pub static VM_ELF: &[u8] =
   include_bytes!(env!("VM_ELF_PATH"));`, with `build.rs` emitting
   `cargo:rustc-env=VM_ELF_PATH=…`).

**Deferred:** the `.boot_image` ELF section + MXBI multi-module archive format
(docs/boot.md). Only one server is loaded in 3.4; a plain `include_bytes!` of a
single ELF needs no new linker section or packer tool. The MXBI packer lands
when Phase 4 loads PM/VFS/RS/etc. Note this deferral in the PR.

Rejected: **bindeps** (`-Zbindeps` — `aarch64-unknown-none` carries the kernel
linker script via config rustflags; fragile to override per-artifact) and a
**wrapper xtask/Makefile** (changes the single-command build contract).

---

## PR 3.4a — Build toolchain + ELF loader (spin-loop VM)

Goal: the kernel builds a freestanding VM ELF, loads it into a fresh AddrSpace,
and schedules it. VM just spins. No IPC behavior change yet.

**User-space toolchain**
- `servers/vm/Cargo.toml`: drop `edition` std assumptions; add `[[bin]]`/profile
  as needed. Keep deps on `minix4-kernel-shared`, `minix4-ipc`.
- `servers/vm/user.ld` (new): `ENTRY(_start)`, base VA `0x0010_0000` (1 MiB),
  `ALIGN(4K)` on every PT_LOAD section (keeps the loader minimal — page-aligned
  segments). Distinct from stub VAs (`0x40_0000`/`0x80_0000`/`0x0100_0000`).
  PHDRs: text R+X, rodata R, data RW, bss (NOBITS, RW). Stack is **not** in the
  ELF — the kernel allocates it and sets `sp_el0`.
- `servers/vm/src/main.rs`: `#![no_std] #![no_main]`, a `#[panic_handler]`,
  `_start` (`.text._start`) that calls `main()` then `b .`-spins. 3.4a body:
  just an infinite loop (proves load+schedule).
- `minix-ipc/src/lib.rs`: real `ipc_send` / `ipc_receive` / `ipc_sendrec`
  via `core::arch::asm!("svc #0", …)`. **Match the kernel SVC ABI exactly** —
  read `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/trap.S` + `user_stub.S` for the register
  convention (which reg = primitive number, which = endpoint, which = `*msg`,
  where the result lands). Mark the asm `nostack`-appropriately; do NOT mark
  `nomem` (the kernel reads/writes `*msg`). `server-rt` stays untouched in 3.4a.

**Kernel ELF loader** (new `kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs`)
- Minimal `Elf64Ehdr` / `Elf64Phdr` (`read_unaligned` from the `&[u8]`).
- Validate: magic `\x7fELF`, `EI_CLASS=64`, `EI_DATA=LE`, `e_machine=EM_AARCH64
  (0xB7)`, `e_type=ET_EXEC`.
- For each `PT_LOAD`: assert `p_vaddr`/`p_offset` page-aligned; reject `PF_W &
  PF_X` (no W+X); per-page `alloc_frame()` (zeroed → satisfies BSS tail for free),
  HHDM-copy `p_filesz` bytes (`mm::phys_to_hhdm`), `flush_icache_range` when
  `PF_X`, then `aspace.map_page(va, frame, prot)` with `Prot` chosen from
  `PF_R/W/X` (reuse existing `Prot::RO_CODE`/`RW_DATA`; confirm/add an `RX`/`RO`
  data variant in `addrspace.rs` if PF combos need it).
- Returns `e_entry`. Signature e.g.
  `pub fn load_into(elf: &[u8], aspace: &mut AddrSpace) -> Result<u64, ElfError>`.

**`vm_bootstrap`** (new fn in `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`, called from
`userland_bootstrap` **before** the stubs so VM = asid 1)
- Reuse the **real `VM_PROC_NR` (=7)** boot slot — VM has a real boot endpoint
  and the IMAGE already set its name/priority/priv. Parallels `build_stub` but
  loads the ELF instead of copying a blob:
  - `AddrSpace::new()`; `elf::load_into(boot_image::VM_ELF, &mut aspace)` → entry.
  - `alloc_frame()` for a stack; `map_page(VM_STACK_VA, …, RW_DATA)`.
  - `alloc_asid()`; write proc slot regs: `elr_el1 = entry`,
    `sp_el0 = VM_STACK_VA + PAGE_SIZE`, `spsr_el1 = 0x340`, `ttbr0_pa`, `asid`.
  - **No priv install needed** — verified: `init_boot_image`
    (`proc/table.rs:247-253`) already gave VM `trap_mask = SRV_T` (RECEIVE ANY +
    SEND any active slot) and a `k_call_mask` filled to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE2`
    (14 bits — includes `SYS_VMCTL` at index 8). Just clear the boot `RTS_NO_PRIV`
    (`rts_flags.store(0)`).
  - `core::mem::forget(aspace)` (tree owned via `ttbr0_pa`, like `build_stub`);
    `sched::enqueue(VM_PROC_NR)`.
- Extend `print_addrspace_summary` to include VM.

**3.4a verification**
`timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release >
/tmp/p34a.log 2>&1`. Expect an `[as]` line for `nr=7 asid=1` (VM) plus the four
stubs; VM scheduled (clock-tick `name[0]` shows it) with no panic / no
`el0_sync_unexpected`; A/B ping-pong + C `SYS_GETINFO` regressions intact.
`cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared` green.

---

## PR 3.4b — Real VM fault loop + kernel→VM page-fault send (milestone)

**kernel-shared**
- `callnr.rs`: add `VM_PAGEFAULT` (e.g. `0xC00`, a VM request range distinct from
  `KERNEL_CALL 0x600`); add a host test asserting its value + distinctness.
- Drop the `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE2` alias (added in 3.3); switch `proc/table.rs`
  fill + `system/mod.rs` dispatch const-assert to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE3`.
- `com.rs`: confirm `VM_PROC_NR`/`boot_endpoint(VM)` usable; add a `vm_endpoint()`
  convenience if helpful.

**Kernel-originated page-fault send** (`kernel/src/ipc/send.rs`: `mini_pf_send`)
- Builds `Message { m_source = boot_endpoint(SYSTEM), m_type = VM_PAGEFAULT,
  payload: faulting_endpoint @0, far @8, flags @16 }`.
- Reuses `mini_send`'s deliver-or-queue core against VM:
  - If VM is `RTS_RECEIVING` from `ANY` (or from the faulting proc): set
    `vm.deliver_msg`, `vm.misc_flags |= MF_DELIVERMSG`,
    `rts_unset(vm, RTS_RECEIVING)`.
  - Else queue the **faulting proc** as the sender on VM's `caller_q`
    (`send_msg = msg`, `sendto_e = VM`, set `RTS_SENDING` alongside the already-set
    `RTS_PAGEFAULT`) — reuses existing caller-queue machinery, no new queue type.
- Called from `do_page_fault` (`exception.rs`) **replacing** the inline
  heap-window resolve (lines ~205-244). Capture `faulting_e`/`far`/`flags` inside
  the existing `rts_set(RTS_PAGEFAULT)` borrow scope, end the borrow, then call
  `mini_pf_send` (same capture-then-borrow-end pattern). Permission faults and
  non-`0x20/0x24` ECs still halt via `el0_sync_unexpected`. Delete the
  `heap_window`/`map_page_in`/`flush_tlb_asid` inline block.

**VM server** (`servers/vm/src/main.rs`, 3.4b body)
- `RECEIVE(ANY)` loop. On `VM_PAGEFAULT`: read `faulting_e`, `far`, `flags`;
  page-align `far`; `ipc_sendrec(SYSTEM, SYS_VMCTL)` with `VMCTL_PT_MAP`
  (target = `faulting_e`, va = page, prot = WRITE); then `SYS_VMCTL`
  `VMCTL_CLEAR_PAGEFAULT` (target = `faulting_e`). Trace
  `[vm] VM_PAGEFAULT from <e> far=… → PT_MAP + CLEAR`. No `alloc` crate.
  (`VMCTL_PT_MAP` already kernel-allocates the frame — `do_vmctl.rs` — so VM
  passes no PA.)

**Stub D revert** (`user_stub.S` + `userland.rs`)
- Rewrite stub D back to "store to its heap VA (`0x0100_0000`) in a loop, no
  SVC" (the 3.2 fault-on-touch shape). First touch faults → kernel sends
  `VM_PAGEFAULT` → VM maps it → D proceeds (no second fault).
- `install_stub_d_priv`: drop `SYS_VMCTL` from `k_call_mask`, clear `ipc_to`
  (D does no IPC). D's `heap_window` stays `EMPTY`.

**3.4b verification**
8 s QEMU run: `[as]` lines (VM nr=7 asid=1; stubs); exactly one `[pf] proc=D
far=0x1000000 …` followed by a `[vm] VM_PAGEFAULT from D … → PT_MAP + CLEAR`
round-trip, then D loops with **no further faults**; A/B ping-pong (`[ipc 1..4]`)
+ C `SYS_GETINFO` (`[ksys]`) all `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.
This is the **Phase 3 milestone**. `cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared` green.

---

## Critical files

| File | PR | Change |
|------|----|--------|
| `docs/plan.md` | 3.4a | Flip 3.3 → `✓ shipped (PR #12)`; flip 3.4a/3.4b markers per merge |
| `kernel/build.rs` | 3.4a | Build VM crate (separate `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, user linker script), emit `VM_ELF_PATH` |
| `servers/vm/Cargo.toml`, `servers/vm/user.ld` (new), `servers/vm/src/main.rs` | 3.4a/b | Freestanding ELF; spin (a) then fault loop (b) |
| `minix-ipc/src/lib.rs` | 3.4a | Real `ipc_send/receive/sendrec` SVC stubs (match `trap.S` ABI) |
| `kernel/src/boot_image/{mod,elf}.rs` (new) | 3.4a | `VM_ELF` embed + ELF64 loader |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs` | 3.4a/b | `vm_bootstrap`; stub-D revert (b) |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs` | 3.4a | Add `Prot` variants if PF combos need them |
| `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`, `com.rs` | 3.4b | `VM_PAGEFAULT`; drop `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE2` alias |
| `kernel/src/ipc/send.rs` | 3.4b | `mini_pf_send` |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/exception.rs` | 3.4b | `do_page_fault` → `mini_pf_send`, delete inline resolve |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/user_stub.S` | 3.4b | Stub D back to fault-on-touch |
| `kernel/src/proc/table.rs`, `system/mod.rs` | 3.4b | `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE3` |

## Reuse (don't reinvent)
- `mm::{alloc_frame, free_frame, phys_to_hhdm}`, `Frame`.
- `addrspace::{AddrSpace::new, map_page, walk_pt, Prot}`; `mmu::flush_icache_range`, `flush_tlb_asid`.
- `proc::sched::{rts_set, rts_unset, enqueue, schedule_next}`; `ipc::send::mini_send` (template for `mini_pf_send`); `ipc::flush_deliver_msg`.

- VM BSS is zeroed by the loader (zeroed frames), **not** by `_start`.
- `flush_icache_range` on executable ELF pages (mirrors `build_stub`).

The plan is written. Here's the summary before you approve.

## Slice 3.4 — Phase 3 milestone (VM server boots, kernel hands off page faults)

**Reality check:** 3.4 is the heaviest slice in the project. None of the user-space build infrastructure exists yet — `tools/targets/` is empty, `minix-ipc`/`server-rt` are empty `#![no_std]` stubs, `servers/vm` is a std `fn main()`, and there's no ELF loader or `.boot_image` mechanism. The existing EL0 "processes" are hand-coded asm blobs.

**Per your decisions, it ships as two PRs:**

- **3.4a** — build toolchain + ELF loader, proven by a *spin-loop* VM. Kernel `build.rs` builds the VM crate (separate `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` to dodge the workspace build-lock deadlock, user linker script via `--config` override) and `include_bytes!`s the ELF. New `kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs` loader; `vm_bootstrap` reuses the real `VM_PROC_NR=7` slot. Milestone: VM appears as `nr=7 asid=1` and gets scheduled, no panic.
- **3.4b** — real VM fault loop + kernel-originated `mini_pf_send(VM_PAGEFAULT)` replacing the inline heap-window resolve + stub-D revert to fault-on-touch. Milestone: `[pf] proc=D → [vm] PT_MAP + CLEAR → D proceeds`, regressions intact. **Phase 3 done.**

**Key findings that shaped it:**
- `vm_bootstrap` needs **no priv install** — `init_boot_image` already gives VM `SRV_T` + a `k_call_mask` covering `SYS_VMCTL`. Just clear `RTS_NO_PRIV`.
- Cross-AS copy **deferred** (your call): every 3.4 copy already runs under the correct live TTBR0 thanks to 3.1b's flush-after-switch ordering; only a stale doc comment gets fixed.
- **Doc fix folded into 3.4a:** `docs/plan.md` still marks 3.3 `◀ ready` but PR #12 merged it — flip to `✓ shipped`.

Plan saved to `~/.claude/plans/lets-work-on-the-tranquil-mountain.md`.

# Slice 3.4 — Real VM Server, ELF Loader, Cross-AS IPC (Phase 3 milestone)

## Context

Phase 3 delivers the milestone **"Boot processes each have isolated address
spaces; VM handles page faults."** Slices 3.1a–3.3 (all merged) built the
physical frame allocator, per-process TTBR0s + ASIDs, a real EL0 page-fault
handler with `RTS_PAGEFAULT`, and the six real `SYS_VMCTL` subcalls. Today the
kernel itself plays VM: `do_page_fault` resolves heap-window faults inline
(`exception.rs:219-244`), and there is **no user-space process** — every EL0
"process" is a hand-coded assembly blob (`user_stub.S`) copied into one page.

Slice 3.4 lifts memory management out of the kernel and into the first **real
user-space server**: the VM server, loaded from an ELF, running its own
receive loop, resolving faults via `SYS_VMCTL`. Reaching it requires
infrastructure that **does not exist yet**: a user-space build toolchain
(linker script, real `minix-ipc` SVC stubs, a freestanding ELF), a kernel ELF
loader, and an embed mechanism so `cargo run -p minix4-kernel` stays the single
build command.

This is the heaviest slice in the project, so it ships as **two reviewable
PRs**: **3.4a** (build toolchain + ELF loader, proven by a spin-loop VM) and
**3.4b** (real VM fault loop + kernel→VM page-fault send + stub-D revert — the
milestone). The cross-AS user-copy rewrite the petal plan lists is **deferred**:
with `schedule_next` (3.1b) flushing `MF_DELIVERMSG` *after* the TTBR0 switch,
every user-buffer copy in 3.4 already runs under the correct live TTBR0, so a
walk-based copy is not yet needed and is left to Phase 4.

**Doc reconciliation (do first, in 3.4a's PR):** `docs/plan.md` still marks
slice 3.3 `◀ ready (branch …, pending merge)` but PR #12 merged it
(`git log`: `64c9548`). Flip 3.3 to `✓ shipped (PR #12, merged 2026-05-28)` and
keep `3.4 ◀ next`.

---

## Build / embed strategy (Option 1 — `build.rs` shells out, `include_bytes!`)

The kernel `build.rs` already shells out to `clang` for `.S` files. Extend it to:

1. Run `cargo build -p minix4-vm --target aarch64-unknown-none --release` with
   **`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$OUT_DIR/vm-target`** (a separate target dir — avoids the
   workspace build-lock deadlock from nesting cargo inside a build script of the
   same workspace) and **`env_remove("RUSTFLAGS")`** + an inner
   `--config target.aarch64-unknown-none.rustflags=["-C","link-arg=-Tservers/vm/user.ld"]`
   so the VM binary links with the **user** linker script, not the kernel's
   (`.cargo/config.toml` otherwise forces `-Tkernel/.../linker.ld` on that triple).
2. `println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=servers/vm/...")` so the kernel rebuilds when
   VM changes.
3. Expose the produced ELF path to the kernel and `include_bytes!` it into a
   rodata `&[u8]` (new `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs`: `pub static VM_ELF: &[u8] =
   include_bytes!(env!("VM_ELF_PATH"));`, with `build.rs` emitting
   `cargo:rustc-env=VM_ELF_PATH=…`).

**Deferred:** the `.boot_image` ELF section + MXBI multi-module archive format
(docs/boot.md). Only one server is loaded in 3.4; a plain `include_bytes!` of a
single ELF needs no new linker section or packer tool. The MXBI packer lands
when Phase 4 loads PM/VFS/RS/etc. Note this deferral in the PR.

Rejected: **bindeps** (`-Zbindeps` — `aarch64-unknown-none` carries the kernel
linker script via config rustflags; fragile to override per-artifact) and a
**wrapper xtask/Makefile** (changes the single-command build contract).

---

## PR 3.4a — Build toolchain + ELF loader (spin-loop VM)

Goal: the kernel builds a freestanding VM ELF, loads it into a fresh AddrSpace,
and schedules it. VM just spins. No IPC behavior change yet.

**User-space toolchain**
- `servers/vm/Cargo.toml`: drop `edition` std assumptions; add `[[bin]]`/profile
  as needed. Keep deps on `minix4-kernel-shared`, `minix4-ipc`.
- `servers/vm/user.ld` (new): `ENTRY(_start)`, base VA `0x0010_0000` (1 MiB),
  `ALIGN(4K)` on every PT_LOAD section (keeps the loader minimal — page-aligned
  segments). Distinct from stub VAs (`0x40_0000`/`0x80_0000`/`0x0100_0000`).
  PHDRs: text R+X, rodata R, data RW, bss (NOBITS, RW). Stack is **not** in the
  ELF — the kernel allocates it and sets `sp_el0`.
- `servers/vm/src/main.rs`: `#![no_std] #![no_main]`, a `#[panic_handler]`,
  `_start` (`.text._start`) that calls `main()` then `b .`-spins. 3.4a body:
  just an infinite loop (proves load+schedule).
- `minix-ipc/src/lib.rs`: real `ipc_send` / `ipc_receive` / `ipc_sendrec`
  via `core::arch::asm!("svc #0", …)`. **Match the kernel SVC ABI exactly** —
  read `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/trap.S` + `user_stub.S` for the register
  convention (which reg = primitive number, which = endpoint, which = `*msg`,
  where the result lands). Mark the asm `nostack`-appropriately; do NOT mark
  `nomem` (the kernel reads/writes `*msg`). `server-rt` stays untouched in 3.4a.

**Kernel ELF loader** (new `kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs`)
- Minimal `Elf64Ehdr` / `Elf64Phdr` (`read_unaligned` from the `&[u8]`).
- Validate: magic `\x7fELF`, `EI_CLASS=64`, `EI_DATA=LE`, `e_machine=EM_AARCH64
  (0xB7)`, `e_type=ET_EXEC`.
- For each `PT_LOAD`: assert `p_vaddr`/`p_offset` page-aligned; reject `PF_W &
  PF_X` (no W+X); per-page `alloc_frame()` (zeroed → satisfies BSS tail for free),
  HHDM-copy `p_filesz` bytes (`mm::phys_to_hhdm`), `flush_icache_range` when
  `PF_X`, then `aspace.map_page(va, frame, prot)` with `Prot` chosen from
  `PF_R/W/X` (reuse existing `Prot::RO_CODE`/`RW_DATA`; confirm/add an `RX`/`RO`
  data variant in `addrspace.rs` if PF combos need it).
- Returns `e_entry`. Signature e.g.
  `pub fn load_into(elf: &[u8], aspace: &mut AddrSpace) -> Result<u64, ElfError>`.

**`vm_bootstrap`** (new fn in `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`, called from
`userland_bootstrap` **before** the stubs so VM = asid 1)
- Reuse the **real `VM_PROC_NR` (=7)** boot slot — VM has a real boot endpoint
  and the IMAGE already set its name/priority/priv. Parallels `build_stub` but
  loads the ELF instead of copying a blob:
  - `AddrSpace::new()`; `elf::load_into(boot_image::VM_ELF, &mut aspace)` → entry.
  - `alloc_frame()` for a stack; `map_page(VM_STACK_VA, …, RW_DATA)`.
  - `alloc_asid()`; write proc slot regs: `elr_el1 = entry`,
    `sp_el0 = VM_STACK_VA + PAGE_SIZE`, `spsr_el1 = 0x340`, `ttbr0_pa`, `asid`.
  - **No priv install needed** — verified: `init_boot_image`
    (`proc/table.rs:247-253`) already gave VM `trap_mask = SRV_T` (RECEIVE ANY +
    SEND any active slot) and a `k_call_mask` filled to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE2`
    (14 bits — includes `SYS_VMCTL` at index 8). Just clear the boot `RTS_NO_PRIV`
    (`rts_flags.store(0)`).
  - `core::mem::forget(aspace)` (tree owned via `ttbr0_pa`, like `build_stub`);
    `sched::enqueue(VM_PROC_NR)`.
- Extend `print_addrspace_summary` to include VM.

**3.4a verification**
`timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release >
/tmp/p34a.log 2>&1`. Expect an `[as]` line for `nr=7 asid=1` (VM) plus the four
stubs; VM scheduled (clock-tick `name[0]` shows it) with no panic / no
`el0_sync_unexpected`; A/B ping-pong + C `SYS_GETINFO` regressions intact.
`cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared` green.

---

## PR 3.4b — Real VM fault loop + kernel→VM page-fault send (milestone)

**kernel-shared**
- `callnr.rs`: add `VM_PAGEFAULT` (e.g. `0xC00`, a VM request range distinct from
  `KERNEL_CALL 0x600`); add a host test asserting its value + distinctness.
- Drop the `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE2` alias (added in 3.3); switch `proc/table.rs`
  fill + `system/mod.rs` dispatch const-assert to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE3`.
- `com.rs`: confirm `VM_PROC_NR`/`boot_endpoint(VM)` usable; add a `vm_endpoint()`
  convenience if helpful.

**Kernel-originated page-fault send** (`kernel/src/ipc/send.rs`: `mini_pf_send`)
- Builds `Message { m_source = boot_endpoint(SYSTEM), m_type = VM_PAGEFAULT,
  payload: faulting_endpoint @0, far @8, flags @16 }`.
- Reuses `mini_send`'s deliver-or-queue core against VM:
  - If VM is `RTS_RECEIVING` from `ANY` (or from the faulting proc): set
    `vm.deliver_msg`, `vm.misc_flags |= MF_DELIVERMSG`,
    `rts_unset(vm, RTS_RECEIVING)`.
  - Else queue the **faulting proc** as the sender on VM's `caller_q`
    (`send_msg = msg`, `sendto_e = VM`, set `RTS_SENDING` alongside the already-set
    `RTS_PAGEFAULT`) — reuses existing caller-queue machinery, no new queue type.
- Called from `do_page_fault` (`exception.rs`) **replacing** the inline
  heap-window resolve (lines ~205-244). Capture `faulting_e`/`far`/`flags` inside
  the existing `rts_set(RTS_PAGEFAULT)` borrow scope, end the borrow, then call
  `mini_pf_send` (same capture-then-borrow-end pattern). Permission faults and
  non-`0x20/0x24` ECs still halt via `el0_sync_unexpected`. Delete the
  `heap_window`/`map_page_in`/`flush_tlb_asid` inline block.

**VM server** (`servers/vm/src/main.rs`, 3.4b body)
- `RECEIVE(ANY)` loop. On `VM_PAGEFAULT`: read `faulting_e`, `far`, `flags`;
  page-align `far`; `ipc_sendrec(SYSTEM, SYS_VMCTL)` with `VMCTL_PT_MAP`
  (target = `faulting_e`, va = page, prot = WRITE); then `SYS_VMCTL`
  `VMCTL_CLEAR_PAGEFAULT` (target = `faulting_e`). Trace
  `[vm] VM_PAGEFAULT from <e> far=… → PT_MAP + CLEAR`. No `alloc` crate.
  (`VMCTL_PT_MAP` already kernel-allocates the frame — `do_vmctl.rs` — so VM
  passes no PA.)

**Stub D revert** (`user_stub.S` + `userland.rs`)
- Rewrite stub D back to "store to its heap VA (`0x0100_0000`) in a loop, no
  SVC" (the 3.2 fault-on-touch shape). First touch faults → kernel sends
  `VM_PAGEFAULT` → VM maps it → D proceeds (no second fault).
- `install_stub_d_priv`: drop `SYS_VMCTL` from `k_call_mask`, clear `ipc_to`
  (D does no IPC). D's `heap_window` stays `EMPTY`.

**3.4b verification**
8 s QEMU run: `[as]` lines (VM nr=7 asid=1; stubs); exactly one `[pf] proc=D
far=0x1000000 …` followed by a `[vm] VM_PAGEFAULT from D … → PT_MAP + CLEAR`
round-trip, then D loops with **no further faults**; A/B ping-pong (`[ipc 1..4]`)
+ C `SYS_GETINFO` (`[ksys]`) all `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.
This is the **Phase 3 milestone**. `cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared` green.

---

## Critical files

| File | PR | Change |
|------|----|--------|
| `docs/plan.md` | 3.4a | Flip 3.3 → `✓ shipped (PR #12)`; flip 3.4a/3.4b markers per merge |
| `kernel/build.rs` | 3.4a | Build VM crate (separate `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, user linker script), emit `VM_ELF_PATH` |
| `servers/vm/Cargo.toml`, `servers/vm/user.ld` (new), `servers/vm/src/main.rs` | 3.4a/b | Freestanding ELF; spin (a) then fault loop (b) |
| `minix-ipc/src/lib.rs` | 3.4a | Real `ipc_send/receive/sendrec` SVC stubs (match `trap.S` ABI) |
| `kernel/src/boot_image/{mod,elf}.rs` (new) | 3.4a | `VM_ELF` embed + ELF64 loader |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs` | 3.4a/b | `vm_bootstrap`; stub-D revert (b) |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs` | 3.4a | Add `Prot` variants if PF combos need them |
| `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`, `com.rs` | 3.4b | `VM_PAGEFAULT`; drop `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE2` alias |
| `kernel/src/ipc/send.rs` | 3.4b | `mini_pf_send` |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/exception.rs` | 3.4b | `do_page_fault` → `mini_pf_send`, delete inline resolve |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/user_stub.S` | 3.4b | Stub D back to fault-on-touch |
| `kernel/src/proc/table.rs`, `system/mod.rs` | 3.4b | `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE3` |

## Reuse (don't reinvent)
- `mm::{alloc_frame, free_frame, phys_to_hhdm}`, `Frame`.
- `addrspace::{AddrSpace::new, map_page, walk_pt, Prot}`; `mmu::flush_icache_range`, `flush_tlb_asid`.
- `proc::sched::{rts_set, rts_unset, enqueue, schedule_next}`; `ipc::send::mini_send` (template for `mini_pf_send`); `ipc::flush_deliver_msg`.
- `system::do_vmctl` (already accepts a target endpoint for `PT_MAP`/`CLEAR_PAGEFAULT`).
- `build_stub`/`populate_stub_slot`/`print_addrspace_summary` in userland.rs (template for `vm_bootstrap`).

## Ordering hazards to watch
- VM ELF must build **before** the kernel `include_bytes!` (build.rs ordering — automatic, but the separate `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` is mandatory to avoid the build-lock deadlock).
- VM BSS is zeroed by the loader (zeroed frames), **not** by `_start`.
- `flush_icache_range` on executable ELF pages (mirrors `build_stub`).
- `mini_pf_send` borrow scoping: capture scalars, end the `&mut Proc` borrow before the rts/queue transitions (CLAUDE.md two-`&mut`-from-one-`UnsafeCell` rule).
- ASID determinism: `vm_bootstrap` before stubs ⇒ VM asid 1, A/B/C/D 2–5.

## Per-CLAUDE.md
- Feature branches `feature/phase-3-4a-…` / `feature/phase-3-4b-…`; PRs (regular merge, never squash); GPG-signed (1Password agent if unsigned).
- Run `claude-md-management:revise-claude-md` before each PR (Phase 3 conventions: ASID invariant, never-write-TTBR1, frame-alloc-not-in-pf-context, the build.rs VM-embed pattern).

# Slice 3.4 — Real VM Server, ELF Loader, Cross-AS IPC (Phase 3 milestone)

## Context

Phase 3 delivers the milestone **"Boot processes each have isolated address
spaces; VM handles page faults."** Slices 3.1a–3.3 (all merged) built the
physical frame allocator, per-process TTBR0s + ASIDs, a real EL0 page-fault
handler with `RTS_PAGEFAULT`, and the six real `SYS_VMCTL` subcalls. Today the
kernel itself plays VM: `do_page_fault` resolves heap-window faults inline
(`exception.rs:219-244`), and there is **no user-space process** — every EL0
"process" is a hand-coded assembly blob (`user_stub.S`) copied into one page.

Slice 3.4 lifts memory management out of the kernel and into the first **real
user-space server**: the VM server, loaded from an ELF, running its own
receive loop, resolving faults via `SYS_VMCTL`. Reaching it requires
infrastructure that **does not exist yet**: a user-space build toolchain
(linker script, real `minix-ipc` SVC stubs, a freestanding ELF), a kernel ELF
loader, and an embed mechanism so `cargo run -p minix4-kernel` stays the single
build command.

This is the heaviest slice in the project, so it ships as **two reviewable
PRs**: **3.4a** (build toolchain + ELF loader, proven by a spin-loop VM) and
**3.4b** (real VM fault loop + kernel→VM page-fault send + stub-D revert — the
milestone). The cross-AS user-copy rewrite the petal plan lists is **deferred**:
with `schedule_next` (3.1b) flushing `MF_DELIVERMSG` *after* the TTBR0 switch,
every user-buffer copy in 3.4 already runs under the correct live TTBR0, so a
walk-based copy is not yet needed and is left to Phase 4.

slice 3.3 `◀ ready (branch …, pending merge)` but PR #12 merged it
(`git log`: `64c9548`). Flip 3.3 to `✓ shipped (PR #12, merged 2026-05-28)` and
keep `3.4 ◀ next`.

**VM server** (`servers/vm/src/main.rs`, 3.4b body)
- `RECEIVE(ANY)` loop. On `VM_PAGEFAULT`: read `faulting_e`, `far`, `flags`;
  page-align `far`; `ipc_sendrec(SYSTEM, SYS_VMCTL)` with `VMCTL_PT_MAP`
  (target = `faulting_e`, va = page, prot = WRITE); then `SYS_VMCTL`
  `VMCTL_CLEAR_PAGEFAULT` (target = `faulting_e`). Trace
  `[vm] VM_PAGEFAULT from <e> far=… → PT_MAP + CLEAR`. No `alloc` crate.
  (`VMCTL_PT_MAP` already kernel-allocates the frame — `do_vmctl.rs` — so VM
  passes no PA.)

**Stub D revert** (`user_stub.S` + `userland.rs`)
- Rewrite stub D back to "store to its heap VA (`0x0100_0000`) in a loop, no
  SVC" (the 3.2 fault-on-touch shape). First touch faults → kernel sends
  `VM_PAGEFAULT` → VM maps it → D proceeds (no second fault).
- `install_stub_d_priv`: drop `SYS_VMCTL` from `k_call_mask`, clear `ipc_to`
  (D does no IPC). D's `heap_window` stays `EMPTY`.

**3.4b verification**
8 s QEMU run: `[as]` lines (VM nr=7 asid=1; stubs); exactly one `[pf] proc=D
far=0x1000000 …` followed by a `[vm] VM_PAGEFAULT from D … → PT_MAP + CLEAR`
round-trip, then D loops with **no further faults**; A/B ping-pong (`[ipc 1..4]`)
+ C `SYS_GETINFO` (`[ksys]`) all `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.
This is the **Phase 3 milestone**. `cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared` green.

---

## Critical files

| File | PR | Change |
|------|----|--------|
| `docs/plan.md` | 3.4a | Flip 3.3 → `✓ shipped (PR #12)`; flip 3.4a/3.4b markers per merge |
| `kernel/build.rs` | 3.4a | Build VM crate (separate `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, user linker script), emit `VM_ELF_PATH` |
| `servers/vm/Cargo.toml`, `servers/vm/user.ld` (new), `servers/vm/src/main.rs` | 3.4a/b | Freestanding ELF; spin (a) then fault loop (b) |
| `minix-ipc/src/lib.rs` | 3.4a | Real `ipc_send/receive/sendrec` SVC stubs (match `trap.S` ABI) |
| `kernel/src/boot_image/{mod,elf}.rs` (new) | 3.4a | `VM_ELF` embed + ELF64 loader |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs` | 3.4a/b | `vm_bootstrap`; stub-D revert (b) |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs` | 3.4a | Add `Prot` variants if PF combos need them |
| `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`, `com.rs` | 3.4b | `VM_PAGEFAULT`; drop `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE2` alias |
| `kernel/src/ipc/send.rs` | 3.4b | `mini_pf_send` |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/exception.rs` | 3.4b | `do_page_fault` → `mini_pf_send`, delete inline resolve |
| `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/user_stub.S` | 3.4b | Stub D back to fault-on-touch |
| `kernel/src/proc/table.rs`, `system/mod.rs` | 3.4b | `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE3` |

## Reuse (don't reinvent)
- `mm::{alloc_frame, free_frame, phys_to_hhdm}`, `Frame`.
- `addrspace::{AddrSpace::new, map_page, walk_pt, Prot}`; `mmu::flush_icache_range`, `flush_tlb_asid`.
- `proc::sched::{rts_set, rts_unset, enqueue, schedule_next}`; `ipc::send::mini_send` (template for `mini_pf_send`); `ipc::flush_deliver_msg`.
- `system::do_vmctl` (already accepts a target endpoint for `PT_MAP`/`CLEAR_PAGEFAULT`).
- `build_stub`/`populate_stub_slot`/`print_addrspace_summary` in userland.rs (template for `vm_bootstrap`).

## Ordering hazards to watch
- VM ELF must build **before** the kernel `include_bytes!` (build.rs ordering — automatic, but the separate `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` is mandatory to avoid the build-lock deadlock).
- VM BSS is zeroed by the loader (zeroed frames), **not** by `_start`.
- `flush_icache_range` on executable ELF pages (mirrors `build_stub`).

toolUseResult.plan:
# Slice 3.4 — Real VM Server, ELF Loader, Cross-AS IPC (Phase 3 milestone)

## Context

Phase 3 delivers the milestone **"Boot processes each have isolated address
spaces; VM handles page faults."** Slices 3.1a–3.3 (all merged) built the
physical frame allocator, per-process TTBR0s + ASIDs, a real EL0 page-fault
handler with `RTS_PAGEFAULT`, and the six real `SYS_VMCTL` subcalls. Today the
kernel itself plays VM: `do_page_fault` resolves heap-window faults inline
(`exception.rs:219-244`), and there is **no user-space process** — every EL0
"process" is a hand-coded assembly blob (`user_stub.S`) copied into one page.

Slice 3.4 lifts memory management out of the kernel and into the first **real
user-space server**: the VM server, loaded from an ELF, running its own
receive loop, resolving faults via `SYS_VMCTL`. Reaching it requires
infrastructure that **does not exist yet**: a user-space build toolchain
(linker script, real `minix-ipc` SVC stubs, a freestanding ELF), a kernel ELF
loader, and an embed mechanism so `cargo run -p minix4-kernel` stays the single
build command.

This is the heaviest slice in the project, so it ships as **two reviewable
PRs**: **3.4a** (build toolchain + ELF loader, proven by a spin-loop VM) and
**3.4b** (real VM fault loop + kernel→VM page-fault send + stub-D revert — the
milestone). The cross-AS user-copy rewrite the petal plan lists is **deferred**:
with `schedule_next` (3.1b) flushing `MF_DELIVERMSG` *after* the TTBR0 switch,
every user-buffer copy in 3.4 already runs under the correct live TTBR0, so a
walk-based copy is not yet needed and is left to Phase 4.

**Doc reconciliation (do first, in 3.4a's PR):** `docs/plan.md` still marks
slice 3.3 `◀ ready (branch …, pending merge)` but PR #12 merged it
(`git log`: `64c9548`). Flip 3.3 to `✓ shipped (PR #12, merged 2026-05-28)` and
keep `3.4 ◀ next`.

---

## Build / embed strategy (Option 1 — `build.rs` shells out, `include_bytes!`)

The kernel `build.rs` already shells out to `clang` for `.S` files. Extend it to:

1. Run `cargo build -p minix4-vm --target aarch64-unknown-none --release` with
   **`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=$OUT_DIR/vm-target`** (a separate target dir — avoids the
   workspace build-lock deadlock from nesting cargo inside a build script of the
   same workspace) and **`env_remove("RUSTFLAGS")`** + an inner
   `--config target.aarch64-unknown-none.rustflags=["-C","link-arg=-Tservers/vm/user.ld"]`
   so the VM binary links with the **user** linker script, not the kernel's
   (`.cargo/config.toml` otherwise forces `-Tkernel/.../linker.ld` on that triple).
2. `println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=servers/vm/...")` so the kernel rebuilds when
   VM changes.
3. Expose the produced ELF path to the kernel and `include_bytes!` it into a
   rodata `&[u8]` (new `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs`: `pub static VM_ELF: &[u8] =
   include_bytes!(env!("VM_ELF_PATH"));`, with `build.rs` emitting
   `cargo:rustc-env=VM_ELF_PATH=…`).

**Deferred:** the `.boot_image` ELF section + MXBI multi-module archive format
(docs/boot.md). Only one server is loaded in 3.4; a plain `include_bytes!` of a
single ELF needs no new linker section or packer tool. The MXBI packer lands
when Phase 4 loads PM/VFS/RS/etc. Note this deferral in the PR.

Rejected: **bindeps** (`-Zbindeps` — `aarch64-unknown-none` carries the kernel
linker script via config rustflags; fragile to override per-artifact) and a
**wrapper xtask/Makefile** (changes the single-command build contract).

---

## PR 3.4a — Build toolchain + ELF loader (spin-loop VM)

Goal: the kernel builds a freestanding VM ELF, loads it into a fresh AddrSpace,
and schedules it. VM just spins. No IPC behavior change yet.

**User-space toolchain**
- `servers/vm/Cargo.toml`: drop `edition` std assumptions; add `[[bin]]`/profile
  as needed. Keep deps on `minix4-kernel-shared`, `minix4-ipc`.
- `servers/vm/user.ld` (new): `ENTRY(_start)`, base VA `0x0010_0000` (1 MiB),
  `ALIGN(4K)` on every PT_LOAD section (keeps the loader minimal — page-aligned
  segments). Distinct from stub VAs (`0x40_0000`/`0x80_0000`/`0x0100_0000`).
  PHDRs: text R+X, rodata R, data RW, bss (NOBITS, RW). Stack is **not** in the
  ELF — the kernel allocates it and sets `sp_el0`.
- `servers/vm/src/main.rs`: `#![no_std] #![no_main]`, a `#[panic_handler]`,
  `_start` (`.text._start`) that calls `main()` then `b .`-spins. 3.4a body:
  just an infinite loop (proves load+schedule).
- `minix-ipc/src/lib.rs`: real `ipc_send` / `ipc_receive` / `ipc_sendrec`
  via `core::arch::asm!("svc #0", …)`. **Match the kernel SVC ABI exactly** —
  read `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/trap.S` + `user_stub.S` for the register
  convention (which reg = primitive number, which = endpoint, which = `*msg`,
  where the result lands). Mark the asm `nostack`-appropriately; do NOT mark
  `nomem` (the kernel reads/writes `*msg`). `server-rt` stays untouched in 3.4a.

**Kernel ELF loader** (new `kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs`)
- Minimal `Elf64Ehdr` / `Elf64Phdr` (`read_unaligned` from the `&[u8]`).
- Validate: magic `\x7fELF`, `EI_CLASS=64`, `EI_DATA=LE`, `e_machine=EM_AARCH64
  (0xB7)`, `e_type=ET_EXEC`.
- For each `PT_LOAD`: assert `p_vaddr`/`p_offset` page-aligned; reject `PF_W &
  PF_X` (no W+X); per-page `alloc_frame()` (zeroed → satisfies BSS tail for free),
  HHDM-copy `p_filesz` bytes (`mm::phys_to_hhdm`), `flush_icache_range` when
  `PF_X`, then `aspace.map_page(va, frame, prot)` with `Prot` chosen from
  `PF_R/W/X` (reuse existing `Prot::RO_CODE`/`RW_DATA`; confirm/add an `RX`/`RO`
  data variant in `addrspace.rs` if PF combos need it).
- Returns `e_entry`. Signature e.g.
  `pub fn load_into(elf: &[u8], aspace: &mut AddrSpace) -> Result<u64, ElfError>`.

**`vm_bootstrap`** (new fn in `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`, called from
`userland_bootstrap` **before** the stubs so VM = asid 1)
- Reuse the **real `VM_PROC_NR` (=7)** boot slot — VM has a real boot endpoint
  and the IMAGE already set its name/priority/priv. Parallels `build_stub` but
  loads the ELF instead of copying a blob:
  - `AddrSpace::new()`; `elf::load_into(boot_image::VM_ELF, &mut aspace)` → entry.
  - `alloc_frame()` for a stack; `map_page(VM_STACK_VA, …, RW_DATA)`.
  - `alloc_asid()`; write proc slot regs: `elr_el1 = entry`,
    `sp_el0 = VM_STACK_VA + PAGE_SIZE`, `spsr_el1 = 0x340`, `ttbr0_pa`, `asid`.
  - **No priv install needed** — verified: `init_boot_image`
    (`proc/table.rs:247-253`) already gave VM `trap_mask = SRV_T` (RECEIVE ANY +
    SEND any active slot) and a `k_call_mask` filled to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE2`
    (14 bits — includes `SYS_VMCTL` at index 8). Just clear the boot `RTS_NO_PRIV`
    (`rts_flags.store(0)`).
  - `core::mem::forget(aspace)` (tree owned via `ttbr0_pa`, like `build_stub`);
    `sched::enqueue(VM_PROC_NR)`.
- Extend `print_addrspace_summary` to include VM.

**3.4a verification**
`timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release >
/tmp/p34a.log 2>&1`. Expect an `[as]` line for `nr=7 asid=1` (VM) plus the four
stubs; VM scheduled (clock-tick `name[0]` shows it) with no panic / no
`el0_sync_unexpected`; A/B ping-pong + C `SYS_GETINFO` regressions intact.
`cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared` green.

---

## PR 3.4b — Real VM fault loop + kernel→VM page-fault send (milestone)

**kernel-shared**
- `callnr.rs`: add `VM_PAGEFAULT` (e.g. `0xC00`, a VM request range distinct from
  `KERNEL_CALL 0x600`); add a host test asserting its value + distinctness.
- Drop the `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE2` alias (added in 3.3); switch `proc/table.rs`
  fill + `system/mod.rs` dispatch const-assert to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE3`.
- `com.rs`: confirm `VM_PROC_NR`/`boot_endpoint(VM)` usable; add a `vm_endpoint()`
  convenience if helpful.

**Kernel-originated page-fault send** (`kernel/src/ipc/send.rs`: `mini_pf_send`)
- Builds `Message { m_source = boot_endpoint(SYSTEM), m_type = VM_PAGEFAULT,
  payload: faulting_endpoint @0, far @8, flags @16 }`.
- Reuses `mini_send`'s deliver-or-queue core against VM:
  - If VM is `RTS_RECEIVING` from `ANY` (or from the faulting proc): set
    `vm.deliver_msg`, `vm.misc_flags |= MF_DELIVERMSG`,
    `rts_unset(vm, RTS_RECEIVING)`.
  - Else queue the **faulting proc** as the sender on VM's `caller_q`
    (`send_msg = msg`, `sendto_e = VM`, set `RTS_SENDING` alongside the already-set
    `RTS_PAGEFAULT`) — reuses existing caller-queue machinery, no new queue type.
- Called from `do_page_fault` (`exception.rs`) **replacing** the inline
  heap-window resolve (lines ~205-244). Capture `faulting_e`/`far`/`flags` inside
  the existing `rts_set(RTS_PAGEFAULT)` borrow scope, end the borrow, then call
  `mini_pf_send` (same capture-then-borrow-end pattern). Permission faults and
  non-`0x20/0x24` ECs still halt via `el0_sync_unexpected`. Delete the
  `heap_window`/`map_page_in`/`flush_tlb_asid` inline block.

**VM server** (`servers/vm/src/main.rs`, 3.4b body)
- `RECEIVE(ANY)` loop. On `VM_PAGEFAULT`: read `faulting_e`, `far`, `flags`;
  page-align `far`; `ipc_sendrec(SYSTEM, SYS_VMCTL)` with `VMCTL_PT_MAP`
  (target = `faulting_e`, va = page, prot = WRITE); then `SYS_VMCTL`
  `VMCTL_CLEAR_PAGEFAULT` (target = `faulting_e`). Trace
  `[vm] VM_PAGEFAULT from <e> far=… → PT_MAP + CLEAR`. No `alloc` crate.
  (`VMCTL_PT_MAP` already kernel-allocates the frame — `do_vmctl.rs` — so VM
  passes no PA.)

**Stub D revert** (`user_stub.S` + `userland.rs`)
- Rewrite stub D back to "store to its heap VA (`0x0100_0000`) in a loop, no
  SVC" (the 3.2 fault-on-touch shape). First touch faults → kernel sends
  `VM_PAGEFAULT` → VM maps it → D proceeds (no second fault).
- `install_stub_d_priv`: drop `SYS_VMCTL` from `k_call_mask`, clear `ipc_to`
  (D does no IPC). D's `heap_window` stays `EMPTY`.

**3.4b verification**
8 s QEMU run: `[as]` lines (VM nr=7 asid=1; stubs); exactly one `[pf] proc=D
far=0x1000000 …` followed by a `[vm] VM_PAGEFAULT from D … → PT_MAP + CLEAR`
round-trip, then D loops with **no further faults**; A/B ping-pong (`[ipc 1..4]`)
+ C `SYS_GETINFO` (`[ksys]`) all `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`.
This is the **Phase 3 milestone**. `cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared` green.

---

|------|----|--------|

- `addrspace::{AddrSpace::new, map_page, walk_pt, Prot}`; `mmu::flush_icache_range`, `flush_tlb_asid`.

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * MINIX 4 aarch64 EL0 → EL1 trap entry + eret stub.
3	 *
4	 * Used for:
5	 *   - EL0 SVC traps (vector slot 8). `vectors.S` branches here unconditionally
6	 *     for that slot; this stub saves user state into CURRENT_PROC->regs,
7	 *     decodes ESR_EL1, and dispatches either `do_ipc` (EC = SVC64) or the
8	 *     unexpected-sync panic helper.
9	 *   - Returning to EL0. `switch_to_user` and the SVC tail both branch to
10	 *     `el1_return_to_user`, which restores `tpidr_el1`-pointed regs and erets.
11	 *
12	 * Calling convention:
13	 *   - TPIDR_EL1 always holds `&mut CURRENT_PROC->regs` while we are running
14	 *     a user context. `switch_to_user` sets it before the first eret; we
15	 *     never clear it.
16	 *   - Field offsets below must match `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`.
17	 *     The .equ block is mirrored from the Rust REGS_*_OFFSET constants; the
18	 *     `const _: () = assert!(size_of::<ArchRegisterFrame> == 272)` canary in
19	 *     context.rs catches drift in the trailing fields.
20	 */
21	
22	    .equ REGS_X_OFFSET,      0
23	    .equ REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, 248
24	    .equ REGS_ELR_OFFSET,    256
25	    .equ REGS_SPSR_OFFSET,   264
26	
27	    .equ ESR_EC_SVC64,       0x15
28	
29	    .section .text.trap, "ax", %progbits
30	
31	/*
32	 * el0_64_sync_entry — entry stub for vector slot 8 (EL0 AArch64 sync).
33	 *
34	 * On entry:
35	 *   - EL1h, SP = SP_EL1 (kernel stack) is live and usable.
36	 *   - All EL0 user regs x0..x30 hold caller values, unsaved.
37	 *   - TPIDR_EL1 = &mut CURRENT_PROC->regs.
38	 *   - ESR/ELR/SPSR_EL1 describe the trap.
39	 *
40	 * We spill x29/x30 to the kernel stack to free two scratch registers
41	 * (x29 will be the frame pointer; x30 is reloaded from the stack into a
42	 * scratch reg before being written into the frame). All other user regs
43	 * are saved directly into the frame via stp/str, then SP_EL0 / ELR_EL1 /
44	 * SPSR_EL1 follow. EC is decoded from ESR_EL1; SVC64 → do_ipc, anything
45	 * else → el0_sync_unexpected.
46	 */
47	    .global el0_64_sync_entry
48	    .type el0_64_sync_entry, %function
49	el0_64_sync_entry:
50	    // Free x29/x30 for use as scratch (frame pointer + temporary).
51	    stp     x29, x30, [sp, #-16]!
52	
53	    // x29 = &frame.
54	    mrs     x29, tpidr_el1
55	
56	    // Save x0..x28 into the frame. x29 (frame ptr) cannot be its own base
57	    // and destination simultaneously, so we save the user's x29/x30 below.
58	    stp     x0,  x1,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
59	    stp     x2,  x3,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
60	    stp     x4,  x5,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
61	    stp     x6,  x7,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
62	    stp     x8,  x9,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
63	    stp     x10, x11, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
64	    stp     x12, x13, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
65	    stp     x14, x15, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
66	    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
67	    stp     x18, x19, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
68	    stp     x20, x21, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
69	    stp     x22, x23, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
70	    stp     x24, x25, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
71	    stp     x26, x27, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
72	    str     x28,      [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]
73	
74	    // Pop the user's x29 and x30 off the kernel stack into scratch regs
75	    // and store them into the frame.
76	    ldp     x16, x17, [sp], #16
77	    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE8]
78	
79	    // Save SP_EL0, ELR_EL1, SPSR_EL1.
80	    mrs     x9,  sp_el0
81	    mrs     x10, elr_el1
82	    mrs     x11, spsr_el1
83	    stp     x9,  x10, [x29, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]
84	    str     x11,      [x29, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]
85	
86	    // Decode ESR_EL1 EC and dispatch.
87	    mrs     x12, esr_el1
88	    lsr     x13, x12, #26
89	    cmp     x13, #ESR_EC_SVC64
90	    b.ne    1f
91	
92	    // SVC64 — call do_ipc(&mut frame), then run the slice-2.5 SVC tail
93	    // (schedule the next runnable proc + flush MF_DELIVERMSG into its
94	    // user buffer). The tail may transparently swap TPIDR_EL1 to a
95	    // different proc; the subsequent `el1_return_to_user` reads the new
96	    // TPIDR_EL1 and restores that frame.
97	    mov     x0, x29
98	    bl      do_ipc
99	    bl      el1_svc_tail
100	    b       el1_return_to_user
101	
102	1:  // Non-SVC EL0 sync exception — route through the page-fault handler.
103	    // It resolves heap-window faults and returns; unrecoverable faults
104	    // panic inside (el0_sync_unexpected is -> !). The tail mirrors the
105	    // SVC path: schedule the next runnable proc (the just-unblocked
106	    // faulting proc, or another), then eret into it.
107	    // Args: do_page_fault(esr, elr, far).
108	    mov     x0, x12              // esr_el1
109	    mrs     x1, elr_el1
110	    mrs     x2, far_el1
111	    bl      do_page_fault
112	    bl      el1_svc_tail
113	    b       el1_return_to_user
114	    .size el0_64_sync_entry, . - el0_64_sync_entry
115	
116	/*
117	 * el1_return_to_user — restore TPIDR_EL1-pointed frame and eret.
118	 *
119	 * Used by:
120	 *   - proc::sched::switch_to_user (first entry into EL0).
121	 *   - SVC tail (after do_ipc returns).
122	 *
123	 * Both paths leave TPIDR_EL1 pointing at the frame. x9 stays the base
124	 * throughout; x8 and x9 are restored last (otherwise loading into x9
125	 * would clobber the base).
126	 */
127	    .global el1_return_to_user
128	    .type el1_return_to_user, %function
129	el1_return_to_user:
130	    mrs     x9, tpidr_el1
131	
132	    // System registers first.
133	    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]   // sp_el0, elr_el1
134	    ldr     x12,      [x9, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]
135	    msr     sp_el0,   x10
136	    msr     elr_el1,  x11
137	    msr     spsr_el1, x12
138	
139	    // GPRs. Skip x8/x9 here — they'd clobber the base mid-restore.
140	    ldp     x0,  x1,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
141	    ldp     x2,  x3,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
142	    ldp     x4,  x5,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
143	    ldp     x6,  x7,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
144	    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
145	    ldp     x12, x13, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
146	    ldp     x14, x15, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
147	    ldp     x16, x17, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
148	    ldp     x18, x19, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
149	    ldp     x20, x21, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
150	    ldp     x22, x23, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
151	    ldp     x24, x25, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
152	    ldp     x26, x27, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
153	    ldp     x28, x29, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]
154	    ldr     x30,      [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xF0]
155	
156	    // Finally x8 and x9 — load x9 last so the base survives until needed.
157	    ldr     x8,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
158	    ldr     x9,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x48]
159	
160	    eret
161	    .size el1_return_to_user, . - el1_return_to_user
162

/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 EL0 → EL1 trap entry + eret stub.
 *
 * Used for:
 *   - EL0 SVC traps (vector slot 8). `vectors.S` branches here unconditionally
 *     for that slot; this stub saves user state into CURRENT_PROC->regs,
 *     decodes ESR_EL1, and dispatches either `do_ipc` (EC = SVC64) or the
 *     unexpected-sync panic helper.
 *   - Returning to EL0. `switch_to_user` and the SVC tail both branch to
 *     `el1_return_to_user`, which restores `tpidr_el1`-pointed regs and erets.
 *
 * Calling convention:
 *   - TPIDR_EL1 always holds `&mut CURRENT_PROC->regs` while we are running
 *     a user context. `switch_to_user` sets it before the first eret; we
 *     never clear it.
 *   - Field offsets below must match `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`.
 *     The .equ block is mirrored from the Rust REGS_*_OFFSET constants; the
 *     `const _: () = assert!(size_of::<ArchRegisterFrame> == 272)` canary in
 *     context.rs catches drift in the trailing fields.
 */

    .equ REGS_X_OFFSET,      0
    .equ REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, 248
    .equ REGS_ELR_OFFSET,    256
    .equ REGS_SPSR_OFFSET,   264

    .equ ESR_EC_SVC64,       0x15

    .section .text.trap, "ax", %progbits

/*
 * el0_64_sync_entry — entry stub for vector slot 8 (EL0 AArch64 sync).
 *
 * On entry:
 *   - EL1h, SP = SP_EL1 (kernel stack) is live and usable.
 *   - All EL0 user regs x0..x30 hold caller values, unsaved.
 *   - TPIDR_EL1 = &mut CURRENT_PROC->regs.
 *   - ESR/ELR/SPSR_EL1 describe the trap.
 *
 * We spill x29/x30 to the kernel stack to free two scratch registers
 * (x29 will be the frame pointer; x30 is reloaded from the stack into a
 * scratch reg before being written into the frame). All other user regs
 * are saved directly into the frame via stp/str, then SP_EL0 / ELR_EL1 /
 * SPSR_EL1 follow. EC is decoded from ESR_EL1; SVC64 → do_ipc, anything
 * else → el0_sync_unexpected.
 */
    .global el0_64_sync_entry
    .type el0_64_sync_entry, %function
el0_64_sync_entry:
    // Free x29/x30 for use as scratch (frame pointer + temporary).
    stp     x29, x30, [sp, #-16]!

    // x29 = &frame.
    mrs     x29, tpidr_el1

    // Save x0..x28 into the frame. x29 (frame ptr) cannot be its own base
    // and destination simultaneously, so we save the user's x29/x30 below.
    stp     x0,  x1,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
    stp     x2,  x3,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
    stp     x4,  x5,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
    stp     x6,  x7,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
    stp     x8,  x9,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
    stp     x10, x11, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
    stp     x12, x13, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
    stp     x14, x15, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
    stp     x18, x19, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
    stp     x20, x21, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
    stp     x22, x23, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
    stp     x24, x25, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
    stp     x26, x27, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
    str     x28,      [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]

    // Pop the user's x29 and x30 off the kernel stack into scratch regs
    // and store them into the frame.
    ldp     x16, x17, [sp], #16
    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE8]

    // Save SP_EL0, ELR_EL1, SPSR_EL1.
    mrs     x9,  sp_el0
    mrs     x10, elr_el1
    mrs     x11, spsr_el1
    stp     x9,  x10, [x29, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]
    str     x11,      [x29, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]

    // Decode ESR_EL1 EC and dispatch.
    mrs     x12, esr_el1
    lsr     x13, x12, #26
    cmp     x13, #ESR_EC_SVC64
    b.ne    1f

    // SVC64 — call do_ipc(&mut frame), then run the slice-2.5 SVC tail
    // (schedule the next runnable proc + flush MF_DELIVERMSG into its
    // user buffer). The tail may transparently swap TPIDR_EL1 to a
    // different proc; the subsequent `el1_return_to_user` reads the new
    // TPIDR_EL1 and restores that frame.
    mov     x0, x29
    bl      do_ipc
    bl      el1_svc_tail
    b       el1_return_to_user

1:  // Non-SVC EL0 sync exception — route through the page-fault handler.
    // It resolves heap-window faults and returns; unrecoverable faults
    // panic inside (el0_sync_unexpected is -> !). The tail mirrors the
    // SVC path: schedule the next runnable proc (the just-unblocked
    // faulting proc, or another), then eret into it.
    // Args: do_page_fault(esr, elr, far).
    mov     x0, x12              // esr_el1
    mrs     x1, elr_el1
    mrs     x2, far_el1
    bl      do_page_fault
    bl      el1_svc_tail
    b       el1_return_to_user
    .size el0_64_sync_entry, . - el0_64_sync_entry

/*
 * el1_return_to_user — restore TPIDR_EL1-pointed frame and eret.
 *
 * Used by:
 *   - proc::sched::switch_to_user (first entry into EL0).
 *   - SVC tail (after do_ipc returns).
 *
 * Both paths leave TPIDR_EL1 pointing at the frame. x9 stays the base
 * throughout; x8 and x9 are restored last (otherwise loading into x9
 * would clobber the base).
 */
    .global el1_return_to_user
    .type el1_return_to_user, %function
el1_return_to_user:
    mrs     x9, tpidr_el1

    // System registers first.
    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]   // sp_el0, elr_el1
    ldr     x12,      [x9, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]
    msr     sp_el0,   x10
    msr     elr_el1,  x11
    msr     spsr_el1, x12

    // GPRs. Skip x8/x9 here — they'd clobber the base mid-restore.
    ldp     x0,  x1,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
    ldp     x2,  x3,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
    ldp     x4,  x5,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
    ldp     x6,  x7,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
    ldp     x12, x13, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
    ldp     x14, x15, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
    ldp     x16, x17, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
    ldp     x18, x19, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
    ldp     x20, x21, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
    ldp     x22, x23, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
    ldp     x24, x25, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
    ldp     x26, x27, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
    ldp     x28, x29, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]
    ldr     x30,      [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xF0]

    // Finally x8 and x9 — load x9 last so the base survives until needed.
    ldr     x8,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
    ldr     x9,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x48]

    eret
    .size el1_return_to_user, . - el1_return_to_user

1	/*
2	 * EL0 stubs for slice 2.5 ping-pong demo.
3	 *
4	 * Two PC-relative-free blobs, each copied into its own physical page by
5	 * `userland_bootstrap` (mapped EL0 RX at USER_CODE_VA_{A,B}). The stubs
6	 * exercise all three sync IPC primitives:
7	 *
8	 *   Stub A — sender: SENDREC to endpoint B in a loop, incrementing a
9	 *   counter held in x19 (AAPCS callee-saved; the SVC entry/exit code in
10	 *   trap.S saves and restores all of x0..x30, so x19 survives the trap).
11	 *
12	 *   Stub B — echo server: RECEIVE from ANY, then SEND back to whoever
13	 *   m_source identifies. Mirrors m_type unchanged.
14	 *
15	 *   Stub C — kernel-call client (slice 2.6): SENDREC to the SYSTEM
16	 *   endpoint with m_type = SYS_GETINFO and payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI in
17	 *   a loop. The kernel dispatcher writes endpoint, priv-flags, init-flags,
18	 *   and name back into the payload; the reply's m_type is OK (0).
19	 *
20	 *   Stub D — SYS_VMCTL client (slice 3.3): self-manages a heap page at
21	 *   0x0100_0000 by issuing VMCTL_PT_MAP / (store) / VMCTL_PT_UNMAP against
22	 *   its own endpoint in a loop. Exercises the kernel's PT_MAP/PT_UNMAP
23	 *   subcalls and cycles the frame allocator. (Slice 3.2's on-demand
24	 *   page-fault path is bypassed — D maps before it touches.)
25	 *
26	 * Each stub carves its 104-byte Message buffer out of [SP_EL0 - 104,
27	 * SP_EL0). SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 rounds to nothing modulo 16;
28	 * we never sub more than 104 so the stack top remains usable). The stack
29	 * pages are 4 KiB so we have headroom.
30	 *
31	 * Endpoint constants below are the boot endpoints of slots 11, 12, and
32	 * the SYSTEM kernel task (-2). make_endpoint(0, p) = p & 0x7FFF, so
33	 * SYSTEM at gen 0 = (-2 & 0x7FFF) = 0x7FFE. ANY's encoding is
34	 * `(1 << 14) - 1 = 0x3FFF` (ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP at generation 0).
35	 */
36	
37	    .equ SEND,         1
38	    .equ RECEIVE,      2
39	    .equ SENDREC,      3
40	    .equ ANY,          0x3FFF
41	    .equ ENDPOINT_A,   11
42	    .equ ENDPOINT_B,   12
43	    .equ ENDPOINT_SYS, 0x7FFE              // boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)
44	    .equ ENDPOINT_D,   14                  // boot_endpoint(stub D); D targets itself
45	    .equ SYS_GETINFO,  0x600               // KERNEL_CALL + 0
46	    .equ SYS_VMCTL,    0x608               // KERNEL_CALL + 8
47	    .equ GET_WHOAMI,   12
48	    .equ VMCTL_PT_MAP,   1                 // SYS_VMCTL subcall: alloc+map a page
49	    .equ VMCTL_PT_UNMAP, 2                 // SYS_VMCTL subcall: unmap+free a page
50	    .equ VMCTL_PROT_WRITE, 1               // PT_MAP prot bit: EL0 writable
51	    .equ MSG_SIZE,     104                 // sizeof(Message); fits in `mov` imm12
52	
53	/* ---- Stub A: sender ----------------------------------------------------- */
54	
55	    .section .rodata.user_stub_a, "a", %progbits
56	    .balign 4
57	    .global _user_stub_a_start
58	    .type _user_stub_a_start, %object
59	_user_stub_a_start:
60	    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent message counter
61	1:
62	    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0): m_source=0, m_type=counter.
63	    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
64	    str     wzr, [x9, #0]                   // m_source — kernel overwrites
65	    str     w19, [x9, #4]                   // m_type   — counter
66	    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_B
67	    mov     x1, #SENDREC
68	    mov     x2, x9
69	    svc     #0
70	    add     x19, x19, #1
71	    b       1b
72	    .global _user_stub_a_end
73	_user_stub_a_end:
74	    .size _user_stub_a_start, _user_stub_a_end - _user_stub_a_start
75	
76	/* ---- Stub B: echo server ----------------------------------------------- */
77	
78	    .section .rodata.user_stub_b, "a", %progbits
79	    .balign 4
80	    .global _user_stub_b_start
81	    .type _user_stub_b_start, %object
82	_user_stub_b_start:
83	1:
84	    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0). Kernel fills it on RECEIVE.
85	    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
86	    mov     x0, #ANY
87	    mov     x1, #RECEIVE
88	    mov     x2, x9
89	    svc     #0
90	    // Reply: read m_source out of the delivered message, SEND back with
91	    // the same m_type already in [x9 + 4].
92	    ldr     w10, [x9, #0]                   // m_source (signed Endpoint)
93	    sxtw    x10, w10
94	    mov     x0, x10
95	    mov     x1, #SEND
96	    mov     x2, x9
97	    svc     #0
98	    b       1b
99	    .global _user_stub_b_end
100	_user_stub_b_end:
101	    .size _user_stub_b_start, _user_stub_b_end - _user_stub_b_start
102	
103	/* ---- Stub C: kernel-call client --------------------------------------- */
104	
105	    .section .rodata.user_stub_c, "a", %progbits
106	    .balign 4
107	    .global _user_stub_c_start
108	    .type _user_stub_c_start, %object
109	_user_stub_c_start:
110	    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent call counter
111	1:
112	    // Build the request at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0):
113	    //   [+0]   m_source — kernel overwrites
114	    //   [+4]   m_type    = SYS_GETINFO
115	    //   [+8]   payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI
116	    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
117	    str     wzr, [x9, #0]
118	    mov     w10, #SYS_GETINFO
119	    str     w10, [x9, #4]
120	    mov     w10, #GET_WHOAMI
121	    str     w10, [x9, #8]
122	    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_SYS
123	    mov     x1, #SENDREC
124	    mov     x2, x9
125	    svc     #0
126	    add     x19, x19, #1
127	    b       1b
128	    .global _user_stub_c_end
129	_user_stub_c_end:
130	    .size _user_stub_c_start, _user_stub_c_end - _user_stub_c_start
131	
132	/* ---- Stub D: SYS_VMCTL client (slice 3.3) ------------------------------ */
133	
134	/*
135	 * Stub D self-manages a single heap page at 0x0100_0000 via SYS_VMCTL,
136	 * targeting itself (ENDPOINT_D). Each loop iteration:
137	 *   1. VMCTL_PT_MAP  — kernel allocs a fresh frame, maps it RW, returns its PA.
138	 *   2. store to the page — must NOT fault (it's mapped now).
139	 *   3. VMCTL_PT_UNMAP — kernel clears the PTE and frees the frame.
140	 * The map/unmap cycle also stresses the 3.1a frame allocator's free-list
141	 * (the LIFO list hands the same frame back each iteration, so no exhaustion).
142	 * D never relies on the slice-3.2 kernel-as-VM heap window (its proc slot's
143	 * heap_window is EMPTY); a stray fault would halt loudly.
144	 */
145	
146	    .section .rodata.user_stub_d, "a", %progbits
147	    .balign 4
148	    .global _user_stub_d_start
149	    .type _user_stub_d_start, %object
150	_user_stub_d_start:
151	    movz    x20, #0x0100, lsl #16              // x20 = 0x0100_0000 (heap VA)
152	    mov     x19, #0                            // persistent counter
153	1:
154	    // --- VMCTL_PT_MAP(target=D, vaddr=x20, prot=WRITE) ---
155	    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
156	    str     wzr, [x9, #0]                      // m_source — kernel overwrites
157	    mov     w10, #SYS_VMCTL
158	    str     w10, [x9, #4]                      // m_type
159	    mov     w10, #VMCTL_PT_MAP
160	    str     w10, [x9, #8]                      // payload[0]  subcall
161	    mov     w10, #ENDPOINT_D
162	    str     w10, [x9, #12]                     // payload[4]  target endpoint
163	    str     x20, [x9, #16]                     // payload[8]  vaddr (u64)
164	    mov     w10, #VMCTL_PROT_WRITE
165	    str     w10, [x9, #24]                     // payload[16] prot bits
166	    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_SYS
167	    mov     x1, #SENDREC
168	    mov     x2, x9
169	    svc     #0
170	    // --- access the now-mapped page (no fault) ---
171	    str     x19, [x20]
172	    add     x19, x19, #1
173	    // --- VMCTL_PT_UNMAP(target=D, vaddr=x20) ---
174	    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
175	    str     wzr, [x9, #0]
176	    mov     w10, #SYS_VMCTL
177	    str     w10, [x9, #4]
178	    mov     w10, #VMCTL_PT_UNMAP
179	    str     w10, [x9, #8]                      // payload[0]  subcall
180	    mov     w10, #ENDPOINT_D
181	    str     w10, [x9, #12]                     // payload[4]  target endpoint
182	    str     x20, [x9, #16]                     // payload[8]  vaddr (u64)
183	    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_SYS
184	    mov     x1, #SENDREC
185	    mov     x2, x9
186	    svc     #0
187	    b       1b
188	    .global _user_stub_d_end
189	_user_stub_d_end:
190	    .size _user_stub_d_start, _user_stub_d_end - _user_stub_d_start
191

/*
 * EL0 stubs for slice 2.5 ping-pong demo.
 *
 * Two PC-relative-free blobs, each copied into its own physical page by
 * `userland_bootstrap` (mapped EL0 RX at USER_CODE_VA_{A,B}). The stubs
 * exercise all three sync IPC primitives:
 *
 *   Stub A — sender: SENDREC to endpoint B in a loop, incrementing a
 *   counter held in x19 (AAPCS callee-saved; the SVC entry/exit code in
 *   trap.S saves and restores all of x0..x30, so x19 survives the trap).
 *
 *   Stub B — echo server: RECEIVE from ANY, then SEND back to whoever
 *   m_source identifies. Mirrors m_type unchanged.
 *
 *   Stub C — kernel-call client (slice 2.6): SENDREC to the SYSTEM
 *   endpoint with m_type = SYS_GETINFO and payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI in
 *   a loop. The kernel dispatcher writes endpoint, priv-flags, init-flags,
 *   and name back into the payload; the reply's m_type is OK (0).
 *
 *   Stub D — SYS_VMCTL client (slice 3.3): self-manages a heap page at
 *   0x0100_0000 by issuing VMCTL_PT_MAP / (store) / VMCTL_PT_UNMAP against
 *   its own endpoint in a loop. Exercises the kernel's PT_MAP/PT_UNMAP
 *   subcalls and cycles the frame allocator. (Slice 3.2's on-demand
 *   page-fault path is bypassed — D maps before it touches.)
 *
 * Each stub carves its 104-byte Message buffer out of [SP_EL0 - 104,
 * SP_EL0). SP stays 16-byte aligned (104 rounds to nothing modulo 16;
 * we never sub more than 104 so the stack top remains usable). The stack
 * pages are 4 KiB so we have headroom.
 *
 * Endpoint constants below are the boot endpoints of slots 11, 12, and
 * the SYSTEM kernel task (-2). make_endpoint(0, p) = p & 0x7FFF, so
 * SYSTEM at gen 0 = (-2 & 0x7FFF) = 0x7FFE. ANY's encoding is
 * `(1 << 14) - 1 = 0x3FFF` (ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP at generation 0).
 */

    .equ SEND,         1
    .equ RECEIVE,      2
    .equ SENDREC,      3
    .equ ANY,          0x3FFF
    .equ ENDPOINT_A,   11
    .equ ENDPOINT_B,   12
    .equ ENDPOINT_SYS, 0x7FFE              // boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)
    .equ ENDPOINT_D,   14                  // boot_endpoint(stub D); D targets itself
    .equ SYS_GETINFO,  0x600               // KERNEL_CALL + 0
    .equ SYS_VMCTL,    0x608               // KERNEL_CALL + 8
    .equ GET_WHOAMI,   12
    .equ VMCTL_PT_MAP,   1                 // SYS_VMCTL subcall: alloc+map a page
    .equ VMCTL_PT_UNMAP, 2                 // SYS_VMCTL subcall: unmap+free a page
    .equ VMCTL_PROT_WRITE, 1               // PT_MAP prot bit: EL0 writable
    .equ MSG_SIZE,     104                 // sizeof(Message); fits in `mov` imm12

/* ---- Stub A: sender ----------------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_a, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_a_start
    .type _user_stub_a_start, %object
_user_stub_a_start:
    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent message counter
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0): m_source=0, m_type=counter.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]                   // m_source — kernel overwrites
    str     w19, [x9, #4]                   // m_type   — counter
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_B
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_a_end
_user_stub_a_end:
    .size _user_stub_a_start, _user_stub_a_end - _user_stub_a_start

/* ---- Stub B: echo server ----------------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_b, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_b_start
    .type _user_stub_b_start, %object
_user_stub_b_start:
1:
    // Build Message at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0). Kernel fills it on RECEIVE.
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    mov     x0, #ANY
    mov     x1, #RECEIVE
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    // Reply: read m_source out of the delivered message, SEND back with
    // the same m_type already in [x9 + 4].
    ldr     w10, [x9, #0]                   // m_source (signed Endpoint)
    sxtw    x10, w10
    mov     x0, x10
    mov     x1, #SEND
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_b_end
_user_stub_b_end:
    .size _user_stub_b_start, _user_stub_b_end - _user_stub_b_start

/* ---- Stub C: kernel-call client --------------------------------------- */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_c, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_c_start
    .type _user_stub_c_start, %object
_user_stub_c_start:
    mov     x19, #0                         // persistent call counter
1:
    // Build the request at [SP_EL0 - 104, SP_EL0):
    //   [+0]   m_source — kernel overwrites
    //   [+4]   m_type    = SYS_GETINFO
    //   [+8]   payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]
    mov     w10, #SYS_GETINFO
    str     w10, [x9, #4]
    mov     w10, #GET_WHOAMI
    str     w10, [x9, #8]
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_SYS
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    add     x19, x19, #1
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_c_end
_user_stub_c_end:
    .size _user_stub_c_start, _user_stub_c_end - _user_stub_c_start

/* ---- Stub D: SYS_VMCTL client (slice 3.3) ------------------------------ */

/*
 * Stub D self-manages a single heap page at 0x0100_0000 via SYS_VMCTL,
 * targeting itself (ENDPOINT_D). Each loop iteration:
 *   1. VMCTL_PT_MAP  — kernel allocs a fresh frame, maps it RW, returns its PA.
 *   2. store to the page — must NOT fault (it's mapped now).
 *   3. VMCTL_PT_UNMAP — kernel clears the PTE and frees the frame.
 * The map/unmap cycle also stresses the 3.1a frame allocator's free-list
 * (the LIFO list hands the same frame back each iteration, so no exhaustion).
 * D never relies on the slice-3.2 kernel-as-VM heap window (its proc slot's
 * heap_window is EMPTY); a stray fault would halt loudly.
 */

    .section .rodata.user_stub_d, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_d_start
    .type _user_stub_d_start, %object
_user_stub_d_start:
    movz    x20, #0x0100, lsl #16              // x20 = 0x0100_0000 (heap VA)
    mov     x19, #0                            // persistent counter
1:
    // --- VMCTL_PT_MAP(target=D, vaddr=x20, prot=WRITE) ---
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]                      // m_source — kernel overwrites
    mov     w10, #SYS_VMCTL
    str     w10, [x9, #4]                      // m_type
    mov     w10, #VMCTL_PT_MAP
    str     w10, [x9, #8]                      // payload[0]  subcall
    mov     w10, #ENDPOINT_D
    str     w10, [x9, #12]                     // payload[4]  target endpoint
    str     x20, [x9, #16]                     // payload[8]  vaddr (u64)
    mov     w10, #VMCTL_PROT_WRITE
    str     w10, [x9, #24]                     // payload[16] prot bits
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_SYS
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    // --- access the now-mapped page (no fault) ---
    str     x19, [x20]
    add     x19, x19, #1
    // --- VMCTL_PT_UNMAP(target=D, vaddr=x20) ---
    sub     x9, sp, #MSG_SIZE
    str     wzr, [x9, #0]
    mov     w10, #SYS_VMCTL
    str     w10, [x9, #4]
    mov     w10, #VMCTL_PT_UNMAP
    str     w10, [x9, #8]                      // payload[0]  subcall
    mov     w10, #ENDPOINT_D
    str     w10, [x9, #12]                     // payload[4]  target endpoint
    str     x20, [x9, #16]                     // payload[8]  vaddr (u64)
    mov     x0, #ENDPOINT_SYS
    mov     x1, #SENDREC
    mov     x2, x9
    svc     #0
    b       1b
    .global _user_stub_d_end
_user_stub_d_end:
    .size _user_stub_d_start, _user_stub_d_end - _user_stub_d_start

1	//! Kernel memory management — physical frame allocator and the address-space
2	//! API live here. Slice 3.1a introduces both; Phase 2.x code did not need
3	//! either (page tables were static `.bss` arrays shared across all stubs).
4	//!
5	//! The frame allocator owns every 4 KiB frame in regions Limine marks
6	//! `MEMMAP_USABLE`. Frames inside the kernel image, the embedded boot image,
7	//! and the static stub pages from slices 2.5/2.6 live in
8	//! `EXECUTABLE_AND_MODULES` and are therefore never visible to this
9	//! allocator — no explicit reservation needed.
10	//!
11	//! Slice 3.1a runs single-threaded with interrupts masked, like the rest of
12	//! the boot path; the allocator carries the same `unsafe impl Sync` boot
13	//! invariant the [`crate::proc::table`] tables document. Phase 3.2+ will
14	//! resolve page faults *outside* fault context (the handler enqueues, then
15	//! the scheduler runs the resolver), so the allocator never runs from
16	//! within a fault path.
17	
18	pub mod frame;
19	
20	pub use frame::{Frame, FRAME_SIZE, alloc_frame, free_frame, init_from_limine_memmap};
21	
22	use core::cell::UnsafeCell;
23	
24	/// Translate a physical address to its kernel HHDM virtual address.
25	///
26	/// The HHDM offset is captured at `init` time from Limine's response;
27	/// all of `[0, 4 GiB)` is mapped under base revision 2 (see
28	/// `arch::aarch64::limine`). Out-of-range PAs will produce a kernel VA
29	/// that faults on access.
30	pub fn phys_to_hhdm(pa: u64) -> *mut u8 {
31	    (pa + hhdm_offset()) as *mut u8
32	}
33	
34	/// HHDM offset capture. Wrapped in the same `UnsafeCell` + `Sync` newtype
35	/// pattern as `kernel/src/proc/table.rs` and `frame::AllocatorCell`, per
36	/// CLAUDE.md's static-mutable-state convention — `static mut` would be
37	/// inconsistent here and trips Rust 2024 lints.
38	#[repr(transparent)]
39	struct HhdmOffset(UnsafeCell<u64>);
40	// SAFETY: written exactly once at boot before any reader, single-threaded.
41	unsafe impl Sync for HhdmOffset {}
42	
43	static HHDM: HhdmOffset = HhdmOffset(UnsafeCell::new(0));
44	
45	/// Set the HHDM offset captured from Limine. Must be called once during
46	/// boot before any frame allocation.
47	///
48	/// SAFETY: must be called exactly once, single-threaded, before any
49	/// concurrent use of [`phys_to_hhdm`].
50	pub unsafe fn set_hhdm_offset(off: u64) {
51	    // SAFETY: caller's contract — single-threaded boot, single writer.
52	    unsafe { *HHDM.0.get() = off };
53	}
54	
55	pub(crate) fn hhdm_offset() -> u64 {
56	    // SAFETY: written exactly once at boot before any reader; the value is
57	    // a plain `u64` so a torn read is impossible on aarch64.
58	    unsafe { *HHDM.0.get() }
59	}
60

content.text: The scheduler is priority-banded and `pick_proc` always drains the lowest-numbered (highest-priority) band first — so if VM's spin loop sits in a higher-priority band than the stubs, it would **starve** A/B/C/D and break the regression. I need the priority constants to handle this correctly. Let me read table.rs.

73	/// Map a [`ProcNr`] to its index in the process table.
74	///
75	/// Kernel tasks (negative `nr`) land in slots `[0, NR_TASKS)`; user processes
76	/// land in `[NR_TASKS, N_PROC_SLOTS)`. Returns `None` if `nr` is outside the
77	/// allocated range.
78	pub const fn proc_index(nr: ProcNr) -> Option<usize> {
79	    let n = nr.get();
80	    let shifted = n + NR_TASKS as i32;
81	    if shifted < 0 || (shifted as usize) >= N_PROC_SLOTS {
82	        None
83	    } else {
84	        Some(shifted as usize)
85	    }
86	}
87	
88	/// Map a [`PrivId`] to its index in the privilege table.
89	pub const fn priv_index(id: PrivId) -> Option<usize> {
90	    let n = id.as_usize();
91	    if n >= NR_SYS_PROCS { None } else { Some(n) }
92	}

content.text: Confirmed the starvation risk: **VM_Q = 4** but stubs use **SRV_Q = 8**, and `pick_proc` always drains the lowest band first. A spin-loop VM at band 4 would starve A/B/C/D. The clean fix: 3.4a's VM does an immediate **blocking `RECEIVE(ANY)`** (nothing sends to it yet → it blocks, leaves the run queue, no starvation) — which also exercises the new `minix-ipc` SVC stub end-to-end and proves the ELF actually executes at EL0 (its RECEIVE shows in the IPC trace). +/// entirely) pointing at `servers/vm/user.ld`, and isolate the build in a

+/// not deadlock on the outer kernel build's target-dir lock.

lines: +/// Load the embedded VM server ELF into a fresh address space, prime its
+/// entry/stack registers, and make it runnable.
+/// Unlike the hand-coded stubs (which use ad-hoc proc/priv slots), VM occupies
+/// populated VM's name, priority, endpoint, and privilege slot (`trap_mask =
+/// SRV_T` → RECEIVE ANY + SEND to any active slot; `k_call_mask` covering
+/// `SYS_VMCTL`). So this fills in only the address space + EL0 entry state and
+/// clears the boot `RTS_NO_PRIV` block. No `install_*_priv` helper is needed.
+/// The `AddrSpace` is `mem::forget`-ed for the same reason as [`build_stub`]:
+/// the page-table tree is now owned via `Proc::ttbr0_pa`.
+/// SAFETY: single-threaded boot; the only writer of VM's proc slot and the
+/// frame allocator here. Must run after `mm::init_from_limine_memmap` and
+    let mut aspace = AddrSpace::new().expect("VM AddrSpace::new failed");
+    // Stack: one zeroed RW page; SP starts at its top.
+    let stack_frame = alloc_frame().expect("VM stack frame alloc failed");
+    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; sole borrow of VM's slot.
+        let p = proc_slot_mut(VM_PROC_NR).expect("VM proc slot in range");
+        // Clear the boot RTS_NO_PRIV: VM now has an address space and can run.
+    // The page-table tree is durable via `ttbr0_pa`; don't run `destroy`.
+    // SAFETY: single-threaded boot; no other PROC_TABLE / RUNQ borrow is live.
/// Build one stub's address space: allocate L0 + code + stack frames,
/// copy the stub blob into the code frame, install RX/RW mappings, and
/// write `(ttbr0_pa, asid)` into the proc slot.

lines: /// ELF entry point. The kernel primes `SP_EL0` before `eret`, so `_start`
/// can dive straight into Rust without setting up a stack itself.
+// `.text._start` is an ELF section name; gate it to the bare-metal target so
+// `cargo check --workspace` on a Mach-O host (which rejects the specifier)
+// still type-checks this crate. `ENTRY(_start)` in user.ld roots the symbol
+// either way, so ordering it first in `.text` is a nicety, not a requirement.

content.text:
This is the 3.4a milestone, cleanly hit:

- **`[as] vm nr=7 ttbr0_pa=0x40000000 asid=1 entry=0x100000`** — VM ELF loaded, entry resolved to `0x100000`, asid 1.
- **`[ipc 1] caller=7 call=2 target=0x3fff result=0`** — VM (proc 7) executed at EL0 and issued `RECEIVE(ANY)` successfully. This is the proof the whole build→embed→load→execute pipeline works. VM then blocks (no senders), so it can't starve the stubs.
